suspension: Telescopic (Front), Swingarm with 2-step adjustable hydraulic shock absorbers (Rear)
description: India's all-time best-selling motorcycle. The Splendor+ is not about performance or features — it is about getting you from A to B reliably, cheaply, and without fuss. Millions of Indian families trust it daily, and that trust is built on decades of bulletproof reliability.
pros: Best fuel efficiency in its class
cons: Design is from a different era

common_problems.cause: Chain stretches naturally with use, especially on Indian roads with dust and water
common_problems.solution: Clean and lubricate every 500 km. Adjust chain tension at every service.

common_problems.cause: Stock foam density is optimised for cost, not longevity
common_problems.solution: Add a gel seat cover or replace with aftermarket seat after 15,000 km.

common_problems.cause: Weak battery or loose wiring connections
common_problems.solution: Check battery health every 6 months. Clean terminal connections.

avoid: Cheap no-name helmets (even if ISI marked)
Overpriced alloy wheels for a commuter
buying_advice.oem_vs_aftermarket: For the Splendor+, aftermarket accessories offer better value since the bike itself is budget-focused. OEM parts are overpriced for what you get. Stick to reputed aftermarket brands.
money_saving_tips: Skip fancy accessories — the Splendor+ is built for utility, not show
Buy during festive season sales (Diwali, Republic Day) for 15-30% discounts
Start with essentials, add accessories as you identify actual needs
Check Amazon, Flipkart, and local dealers — prices vary significantly
common_mistakes: Buying a cheap helmet to save ₹200 — your head is worth more
Skipping regular chain maintenance — it is cheap insurance
Using car shampoo on the bike — use motorcycle-specific products
Not reading the owner's manual for service intervals
